doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_1: The component includes `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` for screen reader announcements.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_2: The `aria-busy="true"` attribute indicates that the content is loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_3: Ensure that loading messages are descriptive and helpful for users relying on assistive technologies.
doc_loadingoverlay_basic_desc: Mask overlay used to prevent interactions while content or pages are loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_choose_appropriate: Use absolute positioning for partial page updates and fixed positioning for full-page operations.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_consider_backdrop_: Use light backdrops on dark backgrounds and vice versa for better visibility.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_provide_context_de: Include a message when the loading operation might take more than a few seconds.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_timeout_handling_d: Always implement timeout handling to prevent indefinite loading states.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_use_sparingly_desc: Only show loading overlays when necessary to avoid disrupting the user experience.
doc_loadingoverlay_desc: A component that overlays the entire screen or a specific element to display a loading state with customizable loaders, backdrops, and messages.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_backdrop: Light and dark backdrop options for different background colors.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_basic: A simple loading overlay with the default spinner.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_blur: Control the backdrop blur intensity: none, sm, md, or lg.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_custom: Provide custom content instead of the default loaders.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_full: Use fixed={true} to create a full-screen loading overlay.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_interactive: A practical example showing how to toggle the loading state.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_message: Display a message along with the loading indicator.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_types: Choose from various loader animations: spinner, bars, dots, or pulse.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_aria_desc: Provides necessary ARIA attributes for screen reader accessibility during loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_backdrop_desc: Light or dark backdrop with adjustable blur effects.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_custom_desc: Replace the default loader with custom content.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_message_desc: Display a loading message to provide context to users.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_position_desc: Can be positioned absolutely within a container or fixed for full-screen coverage.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_types_desc: Choose from spinner, bars, dots, or pulse animations.
doc_loadingoverlay_long_desc: Details and usage for the Loading Overlay component.
doc_loadingoverlay_title: Overlay that covers an entire container to indicate it's being processed
doc_loadingoverlay_design_intent: LoadingOverlay is used to temporarily restrict interaction with a specific area (or the entire screen) during data fetching or intensive processing, clearly signaling that work is in progress. Its goal is to reassure users that the system is responding and prevent accidental operations like duplicate submissions.
doc_loadingoverlay_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the scope of the operation and the desired strength of feedback.
doc_loadingoverlay_choice_loadingoverlay: When you need to completely block interaction, such as during form submission or page-wide data loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_choice_skeleton_when: For dashboard cards where you want users to expect incoming info while maintaining the layout.
doc_loadingoverlay_choice_spinner_when: For minimal spaces like buttons where you just want to show the system is 'thinking'.
doc_loadingoverlay_anatomy_backdrop: Backdrop: Darkens or lightens the background and applies a blur to focus attention on the foreground loader.
doc_loadingoverlay_anatomy_loader: Loader: An animated element that signifies the system hasn't frozen.
doc_loadingoverlay_anatomy_message: Message: Concrete status descriptions like 'Preparing data...' (optional).
doc_loadingoverlay_content_meaningful: For long processes, include meaningful messages like 'Almost finished' to reduce user uncertainty.
doc_loadingoverlay_motion_fade: The backdrop fades in gently to soften visual changes. For short tasks, delaying the display can prevent screen flickering.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_busy: Sets `aria-busy='true'` to inform assistive technologies that the region is currently being updated.
doc_loadingoverlay_scenario_form: Form Submission: Prevents double-clicking after hitting submit while waiting for completion.
doc_loadingoverlay_scenario_init: Initial Data Load: Avoids showing an empty screen while gathering necessary information on page entry.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_1: When you need to block user interaction during asynchronous operations like data fetching or form submission.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_2: When you want to provide visual feedback that a process is ongoing.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_3: When you need a full-screen loading indicator for application initialization.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_4: When you want to overlay a specific section of the page during partial updates.
doc_notification_best_practice_1: Use for information that is more important than a toast and needs to be clearly communicated, such as background task completions or important system updates.
doc_notification_best_practice_2: Provide a clear title and concise description to help users understand what happened and if any action is needed.
doc_notification_best_practice_3: Be mindful of screen real estate; avoid showing too many notifications at once as they might block important UI elements.
doc_notification_long_desc: Display a notification message globally in a corner of the system.
doc_notification_usage_desc: Displays a notification by calling the `Notification` component within your app.
doc_notification_design_intent: Notifications are used to provide important updates or messages related to the entire system or specific features, typically displayed in a persistent or semi-persistent manner in a notification center or screen corner. They are designed to hold more information than Toasts and can be reviewed by the user later.
doc_notification_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on information significance and the user's need to review information later.
doc_notification_choice_notification_whe: For rich information that users might need to read back, such as 'New Feature Announcements' or 'Scheduled System Maintenance'.
doc_notification_choice_toast_when: For transient reports like 'Saved successfully' that only matter at the moment of completion.
doc_notification_choice_banner_when: For critical warnings that need to remain fixed at the top of the screen, such as 'Subscription Expired'.
doc_notification_anatomy_container: Container: The framework that groups notification content, often featuring accent colors based on status.
doc_notification_anatomy_title: Title: Communicates a quick summary of the notification at a glance.
doc_notification_anatomy_description: Description: Details the content or the next steps (actions) the user should take.
doc_notification_anatomy_timestamp: Timestamp: Indicates when the notification occurred (optional).
doc_notification_content_actionable: Make it clear what the user should do after seeing the notification; include links or buttons like 'View Details' where appropriate.
doc_notification_motion_fade: Applies a fade-in or slide animation when added to a list to suggest that new information has arrived.
doc_notification_a11y_region: Ensure the notification list is wrapped in an `aria-live='polite'` region or configured to be announced appropriately when new items are added.
doc_notification_scenario_feature: Feature Introduction: Guiding users through newly added features after an update.
doc_notification_scenario_event: Upcoming Events: Notifying users of approaching deadlines in calendars or task managers.
doc_snackbar_adv_desc: When managing snackbars across the entire application, using a Provider is recommended.
doc_snackbar_long_desc: A notification component that temporarily displays processing results at the bottom of the screen.
doc_snackbar_title: A small message that appears briefly at the bottom of the screen to provide feedback.
doc_snackbar_usage_action_desc: Can include an action button that the user can interact with.
doc_snackbar_usage_basic_desc: Specify the message to be displayed.
doc_snackbar_design_intent: Snackbars are feedback components that appear briefly at the bottom of the screen immediately after a user operation (e.g., delete, move, send). They aim to inform the user without disrupting their focus and provide quick actions like 'Undo' when necessary.
doc_snackbar_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the immediacy of information and the need for associated actions.
doc_snackbar_choice_snackbar_when: To provide an 'Undo' action immediately after 'File deleted' or to place notifications in a thumb-friendly position on mobile.
doc_snackbar_choice_toast_when: For pure system notifications (e.g., 'Saved successfully') that should appear in a screen corner.
doc_snackbar_choice_dialog_when: When explicit user consent is required before performing a major action, like confirming a permanent deletion.
doc_snackbar_anatomy_message: Message: Short text explaining what happened (e.g., '1 item archived').
doc_snackbar_anatomy_action: Action Button: A relevant operation users can take immediately (e.g., 'Undo').
doc_snackbar_anatomy_close: Close Button: An 'X' button for users who wish to dismiss it instantly (optional).
doc_snackbar_content_one_action: Limit snackbars to a single, highly relevant action. Consider a Dialog if multiple choices are required.
doc_snackbar_motion_up: Appears by sliding up from the bottom to intuitively represent a response to the most recent operation.
doc_snackbar_a11y_polite: Typically uses `aria-live='polite'`. Ensure a sufficient display duration so users don't miss the chance to perform 'Undo' or other actions.
doc_snackbar_scenario_undo: Undo Deletion: Provides a few seconds to restore an item immediately after deletion.
doc_snackbar_scenario_refresh: Offline Notification: Informs users when network connection is lost and provides a 'Retry' button.
doc_snackbar_usage_close_desc: Displays a button that allows the user to manually close the snackbar.
doc_snackbar_usage_interactive_desc: You can see it in action.
doc_snackbar_var_desc: Use the `variant` property to apply styles based on state (Success, Error, etc.).
doc_toast_basic_usage_desc: Toasts combine a title, description, and variant (info, success, warning, error).
doc_toast_best_practice_1: Use to notify users of temporary, low-priority process results like 'Saved' or 'Deleted'.
doc_toast_best_practice_2: For critical errors requiring user action or confirmation, consider using Dialog or Alert.
doc_toast_best_practice_3: Keep messages short and concise, and limit the number of toasts displayed simultaneously on screen.
doc_toast_desc: Non-intrusive floating messages that provide feedback on system operations
doc_toast_interactive_example_desc: In the example below, you can interact with a toast to see how it works.
doc_toast_long_desc: The toast component is used to convey short notifications or feedback to the user. It usually appears in the corner of the screen and disappears automatically after a certain period of time.
doc_toast_provider_prop_children_desc: Children elements to be rendered within the provider
doc_toast_provider_prop_duration_desc: Duration for which the toast is displayed (in milliseconds)
doc_toast_provider_prop_position_desc: Toast display position (top-right, top-left, top-center, bottom-right, bottom-left, bottom-center)
doc_toast_system_usage: How to use the Toast System
doc_toast_system_usage_desc: To actually display a toast, it is recommended to wrap your application in a `ToastProvider` and use the `useToast` hook.
doc_toast_title: Non-intrusive floating messages that provide feedback on system operations
doc_toast_variants_desc: Use the `variant` property to choose a style appropriate for the nature of the message.
doc_toast_design_intent: Toasts are used to communicate success, errors, or other brief states from background processes (like saving or sending) without blocking the user's current flow. They provide 'ephemeral feedback' that automatically disappears after a certain time.
doc_toast_choice_matrix_desc: Choose based on the persistence of information and the degree of user attention required.
doc_toast_choice_toast_when: For quick confirmation of success (e.g., 'Saved') where no user interruption is needed.
doc_toast_choice_snackbar_when: For brief notifications that might require a quick action, like 'Deleted' with an 'Undo' button.
doc_toast_choice_alert_when: For important information or settings errors that users MUST acknowledge before proceeding.
doc_toast_anatomy_icon: Status Icon: Visually emphasizes the outcome (success, error, etc.).
doc_toast_anatomy_title: Title: A succinct summary of what happened (e.g., 'Saved').
doc_toast_anatomy_message: Message Body: A slightly more detailed explanation (optional).
doc_toast_anatomy_close: Close Button: Provides an immediate exit for users who wish to dismiss the toast early.
doc_toast_content_concise: Keep toast messages extremely concise since they vanish quickly. Avoid using more than two lines of text.
doc_toast_motion_slide: Appears by sliding from the screen edge and fades out or slides back to indicate its temporary nature.
doc_toast_a11y_status: Typically uses role='status' for polite announcements. Consider role='alert' only for critical time-sensitive errors.
doc_toast_scenario_save: Post-Save Feedback: Confirms that settings or changes have been successfully committed.
doc_toast_scenario_copy: Clipboard Confirmation: Notifies the user that text has been copied successfully.
doc_alert_basic_desc: The most common usage is to display both a title and a description.
doc_alert_close_button_desc: By specifying the `onClose` property, a button to dismiss the alert can be displayed.
doc_alert_error_desc: Indicates that a problem has occurred.
doc_alert_guideline_1: Use to communicate important information without interrupting the user's current task.
doc_alert_guideline_2: Choose the appropriate variant based on the nature of the message (Success, Info, Warning, Error).
doc_alert_guideline_3: For critical changes requiring immediate action or confirmation, consider using a Modal or Dialog.
doc_alert_long_desc: Alert components are used to convey important information, warnings, success messages, or errors to users.
doc_alert_no_title_desc: It is also possible to display only a description.
doc_alert_success_desc: Indicates that an action has been completed successfully.
doc_alert_variants_desc: Use the `variant` property to choose a style appropriate for the type of information.
doc_alert_design_intent: Alerts are used to communicate important information that users need to be aware of, such as the result of an action or a change in system status. They aim to draw attention contextually without completely interrupting the user's workflow, providing either guidance, warning, or reassurance.
doc_alert_choice_matrix_desc: Select the component based on the urgency and persistence of the information.
doc_alert_choice_alert_when: For important information related to specific content or persistent warnings within a page.
doc_alert_choice_toast_when: For temporary feedback like 'Saved' that shouldn't block the user's current task.
doc_alert_choice_banner_when: For high-priority, system-wide announcements that affect the entire page or application.
doc_alert_anatomy_icon: Status Icon: Visually emphasizes the nature of the information based on the variant (Info, Success, Warning, Error).
doc_alert_anatomy_title: Title: A brief summary of what is happening (optional).
doc_alert_anatomy_message: Message Body: Detailed explanation or instructions on what the user should do next.
doc_alert_anatomy_close: Close Button: Allows users to dismiss the alert after they have acknowledged the information (optional).
doc_alert_content_tone: Avoid blaming the user in error messages; instead, provide constructive feedback and clear solutions (e.g., 'Check your network connection' instead of 'Failed to connect').
doc_alert_motion_fade: Applies a smooth fade-in and fade-out animation when alerts appear or disappear to soften the visual impact.
doc_alert_a11y_role: Uses role='alert' to notify assistive technologies of important updates. Consider aria-live for dynamically added alerts.
doc_alert_scenario_form: Form Validation Errors: Displayed at the top of a form to summarize multiple input errors.
doc_alert_scenario_delete: Post-Deletion Warning: Notifies users about irreversible consequences immediately after a delete action.
doc_badge_best_practice_1: Keep badge text short and concise so the meaning is conveyed at a glance.
doc_badge_best_practice_2: Assign meaning to colors (semantic colors) and operate with consistent rules, like green for success and red for error.
doc_badge_guideline_1: Use when you want to subtly emphasize status (In Progress, Complete, etc.) or numerical counts (unread counts).
doc_badge_guideline_2: Can also be used as 'tags' to indicate category classification.
doc_badge_icon_only_desc: You can display a badge with only an icon by omitting the `content` property and specifying only the `icon`.
doc_badge_long_desc: The badge component is used to display status, notification counts, or short labels.
doc_badge_variants_desc: You can change the style of the badge using the `variant` property.
doc_badge_design_intent: Badges are used to highlight subtle status information, notification counts, or category labels without overwhelming the user. They aim to provide contextual information at a glance using color and shape to convey significance or state.
doc_badge_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the nature of information and the level of user interruption required.
doc_badge_choice_badge_when: For brief status information like 'New' or '1' that accompanies an existing UI element.
doc_badge_choice_alert_when: For important information requiring explicit user acknowledgement, like 'Error occurred'.
doc_badge_choice_progress_when: For displaying specific completion rates like '35% complete'.
doc_badge_anatomy_label: Label: Displays numbers or status names. Keep as short as possible.
doc_badge_anatomy_container: Container: Features background colors and borders based on the variant to represent information types.
doc_badge_anatomy_icon: Icon: Small icons can be placed next to text to supplement the meaning.
doc_badge_content_no_wrap: Avoid wrapping text within badges; consider using abbreviations or icons if a longer label is needed.
doc_badge_motion_pop: Apply a slight 'pop' animation when badges appear dynamically to provide a visual cue for updates.
doc_badge_a11y_count: For numerical badges (e.g., unread counts), set an aria-label like '3 unread items' for better screen reader context.
doc_badge_scenario_notif: Notification Counts: Display unread numbers over icons or menus to notify users of updates.
doc_badge_scenario_status: Status Indicators: Allows users to identify states like 'In Progress', 'Pending', or 'Completed' in table rows.
doc_badge_with_icon_desc: You can add an icon to the badge using the `icon` property.
doc_banner_best_practice_1: Use for high-priority, system-wide announcements that require immediate attention, such as maintenance alerts or critical service updates.
doc_banner_best_practice_2: Position banners at the top of the page for maximum visibility, but ensure they can be dismissed once the information is acknowledged.
doc_banner_best_practice_3: Limit banners to one at a time. Multiple banners can cause confusion and information overload for the user.
doc_banner_design_intent: Banners are used to communicate critical, persistent information that affects the entire application or a specific page. They are often fixed at the top of the screen until addressed, aiming to draw strong attention to high-priority updates.
doc_banner_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the urgency of information and its persistence across navigation.
doc_banner_choice_banner_when: For vital messages users must know even when navigating, such as 'Scheduled Downtime' or 'Account Renewal'.
doc_banner_choice_alert_when: For temporary warnings within a specific context, like 'Input Validation Errors'.
doc_banner_choice_notification_when: For system updates where a side notification ('New Message') is sufficient.
doc_banner_anatomy_icon: Icon: Intuitively indicates the severity of the information (Info, Warning, Success, Error).
doc_banner_anatomy_message: Message: Succinct and clear notification content.
doc_banner_anatomy_actions: Actions: Direct links or buttons like 'Update' or 'Learn More' to resolve the issue.
doc_banner_content_urgent: Be concise and honest about what is happening and why it matters, avoiding unnecessary alarmism.
doc_banner_motion_expand: When appearing, use an 'Expand' animation that pushes page content down from the header to signal it as a structural part of the page.
doc_notification_a11y_banner: Ensure proper focus order so users can quickly reach banner actions via keyboard navigation.
doc_banner_scenario_maintenance: Maintenance Notice: Alerting users to upcoming downtime to help them plan their work.
doc_banner_scenario_trial: Expiration Warning: Notifying users that their free trial is ending and encouraging an upgrade.
doc_banner_long_desc: A banner component to present important messages or actions to the user. Typically displayed at the top of the page.
doc_banner_title: Prominent banner for important messages and user actions
doc_emptystate_basic_desc: Combines an icon, title, description, and action buttons for display.
doc_emptystate_icons_actions_desc: It is also possible to combine only icons or multiple action buttons.
doc_emptystate_long_desc: A component used as a placeholder in the initial state when data is empty or there are no search results. It informs the user of the current situation and prompts them for the next action.
doc_emptystate_simple_desc: A simpler design that eliminates borders and background colors.
doc_emptystate_title: Component for guiding users when no data is available
alert_alert_usage: Warnings or recommendations regarding specific situations.
alert_guide_desc: Presents system state changes and user feedback according to urgency and context.
alert_popconfirm_usage: Final confirmation before irreversible operations like deletion.
alert_result_usage: Highlighting results like completion, failure, or 404.
loading_button_desc: Processing within buttons or short-term loading for small areas.
loading_guide_desc: Inform users that data is fetching or processing to reduce wait-time stress.
loading_overlay_desc: When background interaction needs to be temporarily blocked.
loading_progress_desc: Processes where completion percentage is known (download, upload, etc.).
loading_skeleton_desc: Maintains layout while waiting for content to arrive.
guide_progress_std_desc: Defines the role, types, design patterns, and best practices for progress indicators in mobile apps.
guide_progress_role_title: What are Progress Indicator UIs in Mobile Apps?
guide_progress_intro_role: Progress indicator UIs are essential user interfaces in mobile app development. Their primary role is to visually communicate the progress of time-consuming operations to the user.
guide_progress_intro_stress: Waiting times without knowing the progress cause stress and anxiety for users. By using progress indicators to show that the app is working normally and providing an estimate of how long to wait, you can significantly reduce user uncertainty.
guide_progress_intro_perception: Properly implementing progress indicators doesn't shorten actual wait times, but by shifting user attention from the wait itself to the change in progress, you can reduce perceived wait time.
guide_progress_intro_reliability: Progress indicators in mobile apps mean more than just showing progress; they function as an important indicator of app responsiveness and reliability, greatly affecting the quality of the user experience (UX).
guide_progress_types_desc: Classified into two types based on processing time and display method. It is important to select the appropriate type according to the nature of the operation and processing time.
guide_progress_type_determinate_desc: Indicators that clearly show the time until completion. Effective in scenarios where the total amount of work is known in advance (file downloads, uploads, large data syncs, etc.). They visually show progress and remaining time using progress circles, linear animations, or percentage displays.
guide_progress_type_determinate_benefit: Users can grasp wait times concretely by checking the progress of bars or values, allowing them to use that time efficiently. Also, if processing stalls or fails, the progress display stops, allowing for immediate problem recognition.
guide_progress_type_indeterminate_desc: Frequently used in scenarios where processing time depends on external factors. Used when you want to show processing is in progress but cannot specify a concrete completion time (data retrieval from APIs, communication with servers, etc.). Usually expressed with continuous animations like circular spinners or pulse animations.
guide_progress_type_indeterminate_point: The important point is to reliably tell the user that the system is operating and provide reassurance that the operation has not stopped midway. They play a key role in simple but effective feedback that reduces user anxiety.
guide_progress_pattern_spinner_desc: A simple and versatile design pattern. Continuously rotating animations or rhythmic pulse animations intuitively tell the user that processing is ongoing.
guide_progress_pattern_spinner_benefit: The greatest advantage is providing effective feedback while minimizing screen space, such as within buttons or content areas. Suitable for short processing times (2-3 seconds) or indeterminate completion times.
guide_progress_pattern_bar_desc: A linear indicator that horizontally displays progress, clearly showing the total amount and current progress. Features a linear animation that progresses at a constant pace from 0% to 100%, effective for operations with determinate processing times.
guide_progress_pattern_bar_example: Very effective for telling users the progress of processes that take a certain amount of time, like page or data loading. Useful for transitions to pages with lots of images or data, providing reassurance and smoothly filling wait times.
guide_progress_pattern_skeleton_desc: A modern design pattern that displays a skeleton mimicking the actual layout during content loading. Gray placeholders with pulse animations naturally express how content is being loaded.
guide_progress_pattern_skeleton_benefit: Shifts user attention from the wait time to the structure of the upcoming content. Particularly effective for apps with list views or card layouts.
guide_progress_pattern_percent_desc: A UI pattern that shows current progress as a concrete percentage. An effective means of communicating clear progress info for long processing times (e.g., 10+ seconds).
guide_progress_pattern_percent_benefit: By combining a progress bar with numerical (%) display, you can communicate both visual and numerical progress. Effective when users want to grasp concrete progress, like large file uploads or complex data processing. Can also include estimated time to completion.
guide_progress_pattern_custom_desc: Unique loading expressions reflecting the app's brand image or characteristics. Can produce personality and charm beyond standard indicators.
guide_progress_pattern_custom_caution: However, overly complex animations can affect performance, so maintaining a proper balance is important.
guide_progress_bp_intro: By appropriately combining timing, UX improvements, and design/placement considerations, you can achieve smooth operation without stress for users.
guide_progress_bp_timing_desc: Showing indicators too early or too late can cause user stress. Immediate feedback and selecting appropriate display methods based on processing time are important.
guide_progress_bp_feedback_desc: It's important to provide some visual change immediately after actions like button taps or swipes. For operations taking 1+ second, show a progress indicator to indicate smooth processing. Phased feedback, like changing button state immediately then showing a loading indicator, is effective.
guide_progress_bp_duration_1s: No animation needed; state changes are sufficient.
guide_progress_bp_duration_5s: Displaying numbers or percentages for concrete progress is recommended.
guide_progress_ux_estimate_desc: Approximate expressions like "About 1 minute" are more intuitive and provide leeway for development than exact seconds. Setting estimated time slightly longer can create a positive impression if it completes faster.
guide_progress_ux_steps_desc: Displays like "Step 2/5" help users grasp the overall picture and estimate completion. Particularly effective when loading multiple files.
guide_progress_ux_background_desc: Design so users can use other parts of the system during long processes. Use background processing to allow continued work, maintaining productivity.
guide_progress_design_placement_optimal: Place indicators where the association with the action is clear, like screen top/bottom for swipes or near the tap location for taps. Center for global processes, near the element for local ones.
guide_progress_design_integration: Adopt consistent designs with other UI elements, like embedding animations in buttons or integrating progress bars into headers/footers, to minimize user discomfort.
guide_progress_bad_default: Easy use of default OS indicators can harm the app's brand image.
guide_progress_bad_blocking: Avoid designs that completely block all operations during long processes. Provide cancel/pause options to give users a sense of control.
guide_progress_bad_static_title: Use of Static Text or Images
guide_progress_bad_static: Static text (only "Loading...") or motionless images make it hard to tell if the system is frozen or working. Always show movement with animations.
guide_progress_bad_complex: Can consume device resources and cause processing delays. Choose simple and effective ones.
